verdict_reason: The voice for 2.31 is appropriately directive — Krishna opens the duty argument and the engineering layer holds the directive register ('Krishna now opens a second...', 'duties bind you bind you because of the role you actually occupy'). The chapter thesis names Ch. 2 as 'didactic, corrective, slightly stern in places.' The 2.31 voice is consistent with the sāṅkhya-block predecessors (2.22 clothing-simile, 2.30 bridge-coda) but appropriately shifts toward the imperative — the verse opens the karma-yoga block, and the prose registers the shift ('Hesitation in front of it is not humility; it is the quiet abdication of the position you hold'). Sentence-length variance is healthy; no slogan-cadence or hustle-tone drift. PASS.
